architecture.summary: DMoE is a modular knowledge injection system that decouples expert modules and a router from a base LLM. External knowledge corpora are converted into independently updatable expert modules. A lightweight uncertainty-aware router activates relevant experts only when the base model lacks sufficient knowledge during generation. Experts are attached only to the final-layer feed-forward network to preserve KV-cache reuse during auto-regressive inference, enabling parameter-level knowledge augmentation while avoiding full model retraining.

biblio.title: Decoupled Mixture-of-Experts for Parametric Knowledge Injection

claims.statement: DMoE decouples both experts and the router from the base model, converting external knowledge corpora into independently updatable expert modules
DMoE uses a lightweight uncertainty-aware router to activate relevant experts only when the base model lacks sufficient knowledge during generation
By attaching experts only to the final-layer feed-forward network, DMoE preserves KV-cache reuse while enabling parameter-level knowledge augmentation
DMoE avoids catastrophic forgetting, knowledge conflict, and costly updates compared to post-training based methods
DMoE consistently improves answer quality over retrieval and adapter-based baselines on knowledge-intensive benchmarks
